     The Numbers Business Report

In the Heights (2021)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $29,975,167Details
International Box Office $15,200,000
Worldwide Box Office $45,175,167
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $797,227
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $1,162,360
Total $1,959,587
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Lights up on Washington Heights… The scent of a cafecito caliente hangs in the air just outside of the 181st Street subway stop, where a kaleidoscope of dreams rallies this vibrant and tight-knit community. At the intersection of it all is the likable, magnetic bodega owner Usnavi, who saves every penny from his daily grind as he hopes, imagines and sings about a better life.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$11,504,710 (38.4% of total gross)
Legs:2.61 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:66.4% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$55,000,000 (worldwide box office is 0.8 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,456 opening theaters/3,509 max. theaters, 3.6 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $35,251,036

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: June 10th, 2021 (Wide) by Warner Bros.
International Releases: June 11th, 2021 (Wide), released as Ao Ritmo de Washington Heights (Portugal)
June 17th, 2021 (Wide), released as En El Barrio (Mexico)
June 17th, 2021 (Wide) (Hong Kong)
June 18th, 2021 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
June 18th, 2021 (Wide), released as En un barrio de Nueva York (Spain)
Video Release: June 10th, 2021 by Max
MPA Rating: PG-13 for some language and suggestive references.
(Rating bulletin 2648 (cert#52857), 9/23/2020)
Running Time: 120 minutes
Geographical setting: Manhattan, New York, New York City
Plot point: Inheritance, Romance
Subgenre: Musical Drama
Target audience: Hispanic
Source:Based on Musical or Opera
Genre:Musical
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Likely Story, Scott Sanders
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English, Spanish

Synopsis

Lights up on Washington Heights… The scent of a cafecito caliente hangs in the air just outside of the 181st Street subway stop, where a kaleidoscope of dreams rallies this vibrant and tight-knit community. At the intersection of it all is the likable, magnetic bodega owner Usnavi, who saves every penny from his daily grind as he hopes, imagines and sings about a better life.
